title: The Complete Indian Diet Guide to Lose Weight Without Starving
excerpt: Discover how to balance roti, rice, dal, and sabzi while staying in a calorie deficit. A practical guide built around the Indian thali system — no crash diets, no imported superfoods.
author: Priya Sharma

title: How Many Calories Are in a Typical Indian Meal? A Complete Breakdown
excerpt: From butter chicken to dal makhani — we've calorie-counted 40+ popular Indian dishes so you don't have to guess at every meal.
author: CalorieIndia Team

title: Home Workout Plan for Indians: No Gym, No Equipment Needed
excerpt: A 4-week progressive routine using bodyweight exercises, designed for Indian homes with limited space. Includes warm-up routines suitable for all fitness levels.
author: Rohan Mehta

title: Why Your BMI Doesn't Tell the Whole Story for Indian Bodies
excerpt: South Asians have different metabolic risk thresholds. Here's what Indian health guidelines actually recommend — and what to track instead of BMI alone.
author: Dr. Ananya Rao

title: 10 High-Protein Indian Breakfasts Under 400 Calories
excerpt: Start your morning with moong dal chilla, egg bhurji, or sprouted salads that keep you full till lunch — with full calorie counts and prep times included.
author: Priya Sharma

title: Surya Namaskar for Weight Loss: How Many Calories Does It Actually Burn?
excerpt: We break down the calorie math behind Surya Namaskar at different speeds and body weights — backed by Indian sports science research and real data.
author: Kavitha Nair

title: South Indian vs North Indian Diet: Which Is Healthier?
excerpt: Rice or roti? Sambar or dal? We compare the macronutrients, fibre content, and glycemic loads of both regional Indian diets with data from ICMR guidelines.
author: CalorieIndia Team

title: Intermittent Fasting for Indians: Does It Work with Indian Meal Timings?
excerpt: Late dinners, festival feasting, and chai culture — we adapt the 16:8 and 5:2 fasting protocols to real Indian routines without disrupting family life.
author: Dr. Ananya Rao

title: TDEE for Indians: Calculate Your Daily Calorie Need Based on Lifestyle
excerpt: Office worker? Construction worker? Homemaker? Your calorie needs differ vastly. Use our India-specific TDEE calculator with activity levels mapped to common Indian jobs.
author: Priya Sharma

title: Protein for Vegetarian Indians: Getting 100g a Day Without Supplements
excerpt: Dal, paneer, soy, sattu, moong — a practical vegetarian protein plan with Indian food sources, portion sizes, and a full day meal plan.
author: Rohan Mehta

title: Healthy Swaps for Your Favourite Indian Street Food Cravings
excerpt: Pani puri with chia seeds, baked samosas, oats upma — slash the calories while keeping the flavours you love. Each swap comes with a full calorie comparison.
author: CalorieIndia Team
